items.title: How to complete Snaf's Recipe in Gothic Remake
items.summary: Collect some meatbugs and hellcaps for 'tasty' ragout.
items.body: Wander around Gothic Remake's iconic Old Camp and you'll soon run into Snaf, the town cook. He'll give you the quest Snaf's Recipe , which requires you to track down meatbugs and hellcap mushrooms for his ragout. He promises it's tastier than it sounds, but I'm not convinced. Even though Snaf gives you an idea of where to look—which is included in your journal—finding these ingredients is far from straightforward when you barely know your way around. That said, having a map to both the Colony and the Old Camp is helpful. How to complete Snaf's Recipe in Gothic Remake THQ Nordic Mushrooms outside the cave where you find Nek, near the Old Camp south gate. THQ Nordic A cave next to the bridge by the north gate to the Old Camp. THQ Nordic A cave to the west of the Old Camp. THQ Nordic Snaf asks you to find three bugmeat and five hellcaps for his ragout recipe, and here's where I recommend you look: You can find several meatbugs (like large maggots) by the eastern wall of the Old Camp, past your shack and the arena. However, you might already have some bugmeat if you killed some near the abandoned mine at the start of the game. Hellcaps grow inside caves and open fields. You can find f…

items.title: How to join the New Camp in Gothic Remake
items.summary: No honour among thieves.
items.body: The New Camp is one of the three factions you can join in the Gothic Remake . Like joining the Old Camp , it's a very long-winded process. In fact, I'd say it's even more arduous, given all the running back and forth and busywork you have to do. Be warned that you can only join one camp. But if you've got your heart set on this band of thieves and mercs who rule over farmers and miners, touting freedom, then this is what you need to do to join them. How to join the New Camp in Gothic Remake THQ Nordic THQ Nordic The first step in joining this thieves' guild is to locate them. You can find the New Camp in the hills on the west side of The Colony . Head out the south gate of the Old Camp and go west towards the large body of water you can see on the upper left side of the map —the New Camp is right by this. However, the journey over there is dangerous, so I recommend completing a few of the Casting Shadows quests and then bugging Thorus in the Old Camp for work. Eventually, he'll give you the Out of Sight quest , in which he asks you to get rid of Mordrag. Ask Mordrag about his job as a messenger for the New Camp, and he'll offer to take you there, which starts the Admission to the …

items.title: Everything I learned from enduring five hours of Mud in Gothic Remake
items.summary: It's the Adoring Fan all over again.
items.body: Shortly after arriving in the Old Camp in Gothic 1 Remake , you're bound to run into the one and only Mud . He's a lowly villager near the castle gate who'll ambush you for a chat when you walk past his shack. All he wants to do is join you for a while to keep you company, so you've likely said 'why not' out of curiosity. The other option was immediately telling a stranger to piss off, which is a bit rude, don't you think? However, if you've spent more than a minute with Mud, you'll realise just how annoying he is. He'll interrupt you constantly with his comments and follow you around, but when you speak to him, you'll only have the option of telling him to piss off again. So what's Mud's business? Well, I spent hours suffering by his side to figure it out so that you don't have to put up with him. Gothic Remake Mud explained Simply put, Mud is a joke character intended to be a pest and annoy you. The only way to stop Mud from following you is to tell him to go away and beat him up, or have him get killed in combat , though he generally runs away from a fight. However, if the remake follows the original story to a tee, you won't have to worry about Mud past chapter four, if you do…

items.title: Where to find all The Forgotten Recipe ingredients in Gothic Remake
items.summary: Gather all the stew ingredients for Snaf.
items.body: Gothic Remake's resident nice guy, Guy, will give you a free hut once you arrive in the Old Camp. Upon entering your humble abode and looting the chest, you'll find a scrap of paper that gives you The Forgotten Recipe quest. Speak to Snaf, the town's chef, standing by the cauldron near the castle gate, and he'll give you a laundry list of ingredients to find in order to cook this secret stew recipe. Below, I'll explain where to look to get all six ingredients, and how to cook the stew, so you're not aimlessly wandering around like I was. Gothic Remake The Forgotten Recipe ingredient locations Once you speak to Snaf, you will need to find two of each of the following ingredients: Scavenger breast (Raw White Meat) Onions Carrots Potatoes Solanaceae herb Water (Image credit: THQ Nordic) You can get Scavenger breast by killing the weird chicken-like dinosaurs of the same name, found just about everywhere, including in the fields and forests to the east and west of the Old Camp (near the north gate, where you first enter the town). They drop Raw White Meat, which is the Scavenger breast you need. THQ Nordic THQ Nordic THQ Nordic Solanaceae herbs spawn in and around caves. I recommend g…

items.title: How to complete The Vanished Warder in Gothic Remake
items.summary: Nek's gone missing.
items.body: Once you arrive in the Old Camp in the Gothic Remake , you'll need to beat a handful of quests to complete the Casting Shadows quest for Diego. One such task is The Vanished Warder quest, which you can pick up from Sly near the gate. He'll ask you to find his missing friend Nek, which involves speaking to a bunch of people and eventually tracking down the lost warder. I'll explain all of the major steps below so you don't have to waste time searching for Nek in the wrong places—very easily done if you haven't immediately purchased a map . How to complete The Vanished Warder in Gothic Remake (Image credit: THQ Nordic) Once you speak to Sly to start The Vanished Warder quest, he'll suggest you speak to Fletcher, a warder by the arena and your shack on the left side of the Old Camp. Fletcher sends you to talk to Guy, a miner in the same area, who passes you on to Snaf, the chef to the left of the gates. Eventually, you'll learn that Snaf sent Nek out to find hellcap mushrooms. You don't need to speak to these characters in order to find Nek , but it is a good excuse to learn more about the Old Camp and its inhabitants. In fact, my quest bugged and I couldn't speak to Guy or Snaf abou…

items.title: How to get armour in Gothic Remake
items.summary: New armour is few and far between.
items.body: One of the most captivating parts of Gothic Remake is that you start off as a poor sod with no gear to your name, and everyone's trying to swindle you. Unlike basically every other RPG ever, you don't even start with a basic weapon, so even little critters will eat you for breakfast. Naturally, finding out how to get armour is your next milestone after grabbing a sword. I also recommend completing The Price of Magic quest early on to unlock magic, which makes early combat (especially without armour) much more manageable. How to get armour in Gothic Remake (Image credit: THQ Nordic) Getting armour is primarily tied to your faction progression of the three main camps or sold by vendors —you generally don't find any from exploring, unlike weapons. At the start of the game, you won't have any armour, just the Nameless Hero's boring black outfit (which isn't even considered an item). New gear sets are very few and far between, but you can grab one for free relatively early on. (Image credit: THQ Nordic) The first armour you'll be able to get is the Digger's Light Trousers by purchasing them from Fisk in the Old Camp marketplace for 313 ore . He's got short black hair and is wearing the…

items.title: Can you stow your torch in Gothic Remake?
items.summary: I guess I wouldn't want to pocket a burning object either.
items.body: One of the first things you're likely to realise in Gothic Remake is how utterly annoying its torch system feels. When you activate a torch in your hotbar, you'll light it up, which is helpful in such an often gloomy game, but there's no way to put it back in your inventory while it's still burning. If you select a sword, for example, you'll stab the torch into the ground where it'll stay burning for a good while. You can either pick it up and continue, or wait for it to burn out. Admittedly, it feels quite cool stabbing a torch into the ground in some cave so you can draw your sword and fight a bunch of goblins, but for exploration it's a pain. There is simply no key or input that puts your torch away once it's lit. In fact, the only way your character will stow a torch is if you walk into water and start swimming to snuff the flame. Otherwise, your only option is waiting for it to burn out and collecting it later. There are a couple of handy exceptions though: If you rest in a bed , your character will stab the torch into the ground, and it will have gone out by the time you wake up If you stab a torch into a puddle or shallow pool of water, it will instantly go out Thankfully, …

items.title: Learning magic early in Gothic Remake saved me from constantly getting mauled by wolves
items.summary: Now that's some good shi- I mean 'mana'.
items.body: The Gothic Remake is a classic RPG, sure, but mostly it's really a game about getting your ass kicked in a hundred different ways. When I waltzed up to the gate of the Old Camp and tried to crack a joke with the guard like I was some cocky protagonist in any other fantasy adventure, he beat me up and took all my stuff. It was a memorable lesson, though not enough to prevent the exact same thing happening at the next gate I came to. I like sassing guards, what can I say? Over the proceeding hours I found myself using every trick in the book to get by, killing "meat" bugs for food, filching whatever wasn't nailed down, but frequently getting murdered by wolves, scavengers, and even molerats. Combat in Gothic is so hard because there's so little room for error. Enemies deal a ton of damage, so any mistake is fatal until you find some armor . Since this gear is so valuable in the Valley of the Mines, you're not just going to wander into some cave Elder Scrolls-style and find a full suit in a random chest—it's mainly tied to quests and faction progress. The same goes for weaponry—if you want a really good weapon you'll most likely have to take it from someone else, something I realised…

items.title: How to pass time in Gothic Remake
items.summary: You'll have to find an unoccupied bed to change the time of day.
items.body: You'll likely want to pass time when you first arrive in the Old Camp in Gothic Remake . If you're anything like me, you likely spent the whole first day exploring bits and bobs, so when you arrived at the main camp, it was already starting to get a bit gloomy and all of the town's NPCs were tucked up in bed. You might've tried to sleep in a bed to pass time, only to be rudely awoken by its owner, wondering why you're taking 20 winks in his shack. Well, the good news is that there's a bed and a house just for you in the Old Camp, and all you have to do is find them, which I'll walk you through below. How to pass time in Gothic Remake You can pass time in Gothic by sleeping in a bed , but as with other RPGs, it can't be an owned bed. Whenever you try to sleep in a bed belonging to someone else (the sleep icon will be red), they will wake you up shortly after you nod off and won't be best pleased. Thankfully, though, you can get a free shack and a bed as soon as you arrive in the Old Camp near the start of the game. You can see how to get to the location in the video above. If you can't see it, then from the north entrance to the Old Camp (which you enter via when you first arrive),…

items.title: How to get the Scavenger mount in Gothic Remake
items.summary: Acquire Kingstide and Wolf Pelts to tame and saddle this big bird.
items.body: Taming a Scavenger mount in the Gothic Remake is great if you don't want to walk around the entire map on foot, or retread the same old routes over and over. While you can unlock fast travel in Gothic, it's limited to camps, so getting a mount speeds things up significantly in terms of travelling between known locales. You can get the Scavenger mount at any time during the game, but you will need enough money and learning points (detailed below) to actually acquire the mount, some skills, and make the saddle. All that said, here's how to saddle up a Scavenger to ride. How to unlock the Scavenger mount in Gothic Remake You'll have to complete the Beak Performance quest that you get from Cohsel to the west of the Old Camp to get the Scavenger mount. Before you start, be warned that you'll need at least 280 ore nuggets and six learning points . The location is shown in the video above, but basically: Head out the north gate of the Old Camp and turn left till you hit a path running south When you reach the crossroads with the sign, turn right on the path descending Follow the path through the delve, passing Cavalorn's hut on the right Ascend out the other side, turning left, to spot C…

items.title: You can only pick one faction in Gothic Remake, so which one should you choose?
items.summary: The Old Camp, New Camp, or Swamp Camp.
items.body: The best part of Gothic Remake 's opening chapter is juggling the three factions : the Old Camp, New Camp, and Swamp Camp. Each of the three camps dislikes the others, but they're forced to work together somewhat to survive in The Colony. And then there's you, the Nameless Hero and a fresh face, who must pick a side. But which camp is right for you? Which camp should you choose in Gothic Remake? You can only join one faction, and there are some consequences to your actions. The good news is that you will still be able to visit and even complete some quests in the other camps , so it's not the end of the world. In fact, you'll even continue to receive the free daily items, like the ragout from the Old Camp or swampweed from the Swamp Camp. There's no bad blood if you're following the rules. All factions are, frankly, kind of assholes, so it's more about picking the least nasty team and whichever one's theme is the coolest to you: hierarchical soldiers, a band of thieves and mercs, or cultists. The main impacts of your faction choice include: Your quests are based on which faction you choose , mainly in the first chapter. You need to deliver a message to the fire mages, but you can …

items.title: How to get a map in Gothic Remake
items.summary: Stop getting lost exploring The Colony with a little help.
items.body: Finding and remembering where stuff is in Gothic Remake can be a bit of a pain, especially as there's no compass. In fact, if you want a map , then you'll have to acquire one yourself with your hard-earned ore nuggets . It won't mark important NPCs or locations, but it does give you a useful lay of the land, whether you're in a settlement like the Old Camp or exploring the wider colony region. You can also add markers to the map yourself, helping you remember where certain points of interest are from moment to moment. All that said, here's how to get a map in Gothic 1 Remake, a little tip to help you get one for cheap, and whether buying every map is strictly necessary. How to get a map in Gothic Remake You can purchase a map once you arrive in the Old Camp, and you have two separate options. Graham, the map maker, sells both a Colony map and an Old Camp map, for the wider world and the settlement respectively, but you can actually get the Colony map at a lower price if you buy it from Dexter in the market area. The video above shows you how to find both Graham and Dexter. Graham's shack is right next to the north gate (where you originally enter the Old Camp) and you can find him…

items.title: How to complete The Price of Magic in Gothic Remake
items.summary: Find one Parchment and two Wolf's Claws for Torrez.
items.body: Magic is very strong in Gothic Remake , especially early on in the game, where it can add a big boost to your firepower. This is why it's a good idea to complete The Price of Magic quest in the Old Camp. All you need to do is find a couple of items for the mage Torrez, and in return, he'll give you a valuable spell rune which lets you cast permanently so long as you have mana in the tank. This will also unlock his magical inventory, letting you purchase more spells, including one-time consumable scrolls, though these will be well beyond your price range early on. Still, offensive magic will let you punch well above your weight, so here's how to complete the quest. How to complete The Price of Magic in Gothic Remake You find buy Parchment from Graham by the north gate Alkimia Interactive It'll cost you 13 ore Alkimia Interactive You'll meet the mage Torrez in the southwest of the Old Camp near the big market area covered by hanging cloth. This is the same area with Fisk, Dexter, and Mordrag. To get here, simply: Head to the castle entrance from the north gate Take a right down past the area with the water and Gravo's shack Keep going and you'll find Torrez on the right by the well,…

items.title: How to complete The Cult's Recipe in Gothic Remake
items.summary: Snatch Cor Kalom's recipe for Dexter.
items.body: Completing The Cult's Recipe in Gothic Remake is one of the necessary steps in impressing Diego enough to get you a recommendation to join the Old Camp . In this quest, Dexter asks you to head all the way to the Swamp Camp and steal Cor Kalom's recipe for creating mystical hallucinogens. The trickiest part of this quest is actually getting to the Swamp Camp, since it's a dangerous route filled with enemies who'll kill you in one hit thanks to your lack of armor . So, here's how to get there safely, find the recipe, and even lockpick the chest containing it. How to get to the Swamp Camp in Gothic Remake Speak to Baal Parvez in the same market area as Dexter and he'll take you to the Swamp Camp (Image credit: Alkimia Interactive) After Dexter tasks you with stealing Cor Kalom's recipe, you'll have to find a way to the Swamp Camp. Before you do, though, make sure you have a couple of lockpicks —you can buy these from Mordrag in the same market area as Dexter if you head east past the fire pit towards the arena and look for him to your left, sitting on a bench in front of a shack. I'd also recommend grabbing a map of the Colony. Now, you'll want to speak to Baal Parvez , the cultist w…

items.title: How to earn ore in Gothic Remake
items.summary: Get rich and grab yourself some half-decent gear.
items.body: Acquiring ore nuggets in the Gothic Remake can be a taxing process, quite literally as it happens, as warders squeeze you for protection money and you have to shell out for even the most basic gear to keep yourself alive. Protective armor , decent weaponry, and even magic are all luxuries in the Valley of the Mines and will cost you a pretty penny to gather. Thankfully, there are some ways to make money, though many of them require a little investment to begin with, specifically in learning skills. You can even mine ore if you want a basic way to accrue currency early on, but it's worth investing in more lucrative methods as you progress further into the game. Best ways to earn ore in Gothic Remake It's advisable early on to grab basically everything you find in order to sell it, especially herbs and mushrooms, which can be surprisingly valuable. You'll need all the ore you can get to buy a map and equip yourself with gear that isn't total trash. Thankfully, before long you should start to steamroll in ore terms, especially if you follow the main quest and clear the dungeons in it like the goblin cave and orc cemetery. All of these humanoid enemies have weapons that are valuable t…

items.title: Where to find Bran to complete Up to His Neck in Gothic Remake
items.summary: Search around the New Camp to find Bran, the Rice Lord's second in command.
items.body: Joining the New Camp is a very long-winded process in Gothic Remake . Alongside handing out a bunch of swampweed for cash, you also need to help the Rice Lord. And after completing the A Few Drops Too Much quest, this so-called lord of rice will set you the task of finding Bran in the Up to His Neck quest. Take note that while this quest will earn you the trust of the Rice Lord, a major step in joining the New Camp, you'll make that decision with Lares later. In other words, it won't lock you out of joining the other camp factions . Before you start this quest, I also recommend grabbing some armour , especially the free Digger's Dress. How to complete Up to His Neck in Gothic Remake (Image credit: THQ Nordic) The Rice Lord needs you to find Bran, his missing foreman, and tells you to speak to the farmers to find out more. Only the named farmers will comment on Bran, but, at least for me, Malon told me where he saw him disappear to. You need to speak to Malon kindly . Malon explains that Bran went to a cave near the pond where Jacko and his crew hang around. To save you from running around the camp for ages like I did (there's a pond and lake in there, after all), to find the corre…

items.title: How to complete Casting Shadows and join the Old Camp in Gothic Remake
items.summary: Get Diego's help to enter the castle and convince Gomez.
items.body: Trying to join the Old Camp in Gothic Remake is a surprisingly long process, requiring you to earn the trust of important members of the faction through a bucketload of quests and tasks. It's nothing like joining the Fighter's Guild in an Elder Scrolls game, where you can just rock up and start killing rats. There are two quests involved in joining the Old Camp, Admission to the Old Camp and Casting Shadows , the latter of which is the most important, as you convince the shadows to support your candidacy in joining the faction. So, here's everything you need to do to complete that quest and become a member of the Old Camp. How to complete Casting Shadows in Gothic Remake Speak to Thorus at the castle gate and then to Diego by his shack just next to it (Image credit: Alkimia Interactive) After you arrive in the Old Camp and speak to Thorus at the castle gate, he'll explain that if you want to get inside and join the faction, you need someone to vouch for you, namely, Diego, who you met at the start of the game. Diego's shack is right by the castle gate, and upon speaking to him, he'll give you the Casting Shadows quest, which is all about making a good impression on the Shadows—the…

items.title: How to lockpick and get lockpicks in Gothic Remake
items.summary: Well, you are a criminal after all.
items.body: I'm not sure I've come across a lockpicking minigame that feels quite as brutal as the one in Gothic Remake , especially when you're untrained. I guess it's probably quite similar to how it would feel if I gave you a lockpick and asked you to open a locked chest in real life (assuming you're not some kind of master thief on the sly). Luckily, like most things in Gothic, it becomes significantly easier if you invest in training and gather plenty of lockpicks to use when you're intent on cracking open a container and filching some items. So, here are the fundamentals of lockpicking, how to make it easier, and where to get more picks. How to lockpick in Gothic Remake Gothic has some of the hardest lockpicking I've encountered in an RPG, especially when tackling complex locks with six sliders. You can see me lockpicking a chest during The Cult's Recipe quest above. Otherwise, here's a brief rundown of how it works: Your goal is to align all of the sliders : Each slider has seven holes and your job is to align them all (by selecting and moving each) so the copper-coloured pin is in the middle hole for each of them. When a slider is correctly aligned, the pin will stick out of the hole.…

items.title: The Tomb Raider: Legacy of Atlantis system requirements are here—even if the game just got further away
items.summary: Better than nothing, I guess.
items.body: We've known about Tomb Raider: Legacy of Atlantis for a little while now , but with a new trailer being shown off at Sony's State of Play, we got a fresh new update on the game. Unfortunately, it's also been delayed through to 2027—but we now have the system requirements at the very least. The good news is, for a game launching in 2027, most of its specs are pretty breezy to run the game at minimum. You will need either an Intel i5 8600 or an AMD Ryzen 5 3600 paired with either an 8 GB GTX 1070 , RTX 2060 Super , or AMD RX 5700 . Given that the 2016 GTX 1070 goes for $100 or less second-hand now, and the 2019 Ryzen 5 3600 was pretty reasonably priced even when it launched, there's a good chance a five-year-old budget to mid-range rig should stand a chance of running it well. The one sticking point for some will be its memory requirement, which is 16 GB. That's not exactly high or anything, but with the memory crisis in full swing, it's not a cheap upgrade for those of you stuck on 8 GB. We also don't yet know what minimum means in this context. It could mean the lowest graphical settings at 1080p, 30 fps, or maybe 60 fps. It could be just generally 'playable', as defined by the de…

items.title: These are the 5 games you need to check out after watching A24's Backrooms film
items.summary: Backrooms is a state of mind.
items.body: If you're anything like me, you probably came out of A24's Backrooms film thinking to yourself: 'Scary movie, but I could've survived it'. Well, you can put that to the test, in theory. While I don't recommend running around any derelict office buildings, there are hundreds of backrooms games and others like it for you to check out. The only problem is, however, that there are hundreds of games; it can be pretty difficult to sort through the masses to find the diamond in the rough. So to try and help jump start your search here are the five games I think you should check out if you enjoyed the vibes Backrooms film. Escape the Backrooms (Image credit: Fancy Games) This is the most obvious choice for any backrooms enjoyers. Escape the Backrooms one of, if not the, most popular backrooms games around, and for good reason. With over 30 levels to explore and multiple entities to try and survive, it captures the now-iconic vibes. But the best way to experience this game is with friends. It does shift the vibe ever so slightly, from eerie to chaos, but it's worth it. I've spent hours running around all the different levels with my friends, as we try and ultimately fail to escape whatever…

To be fair to the HP Limited Edition Scuderia Ferrari AI PC, it is a looker with a very snazzy 3K tandem OLED+ touch display. Beyond that, the rather fetching red chassis "marries carbon fiber and Corning Gorilla Glass construction." The underside of the laptop is partially see-through too; the cooling system in particular is visible "as a direct nod to Ferrari’s exposed engine bay design". HP's senior vice president of design and sustainability, Stacy Wolff, bills …

items.title: Where to find Shibuya Crossing in Forza Horizon 6 and beat Tokyo Drift
items.summary: One of the world's busiest streets, now without pedestrians.
items.body: Tokyo Drift is one of the most annoying Series 1 daily challenges in Forza Horizon 6 . Not only do you need to find Shibuya Crossing , but you also need to perform three Awesome Drifts, which is easier said than done. I'll explain where to find Shibuya Crossing below, alongside how best to complete this pesky challenge so you can get your Playlist Festival points and put this task in the rearview mirror. Forza Horizon 6 Shibuya Crossing location Xbox Xbox Shibuya Crossing is a four-way junction in the northwest of Tokyo City , almost directly south of the Highway Circuit road race, and east of the food delivery job icon. It's not far from Edogawa Baseball Stadium , which you also need to visit this week for the Pitcher Perfect challenge. You'll recognise Shibuya Crossing by the many road crossings, but also the massive advertisements on the side of the buildings. There are Horizon Festival barriers all around the crossroads, too. How to beat Tokyo Drift in Forza Horizon 6 As for how to beat Tokyo Drift, the issue with this challenge is how vague the description is. items.title: Godot devs are getting a helping hand from Microsoft to bring their games to Xbox on PC
items.summary: It's only a little step forward, but at least it's in the right direction.
items.body: Although many developers turn to Unreal Engine or Unity to bring their gaming visions to life, open-source Godot is becoming increasingly popular, thanks to its lightweight structure and intuitive scripting language. Now Microsoft has taken a 'first step' to expanding its user base even further, by releasing a reference sample software package for creating Godot games for Xbox on PC. Yes, I know I should be writing XBOX on PC, given that Microsoft has decided to settle on the format , but I'll be writing Xbox until I'm in the grave. Anyway, nomenclature aside, it should be noted that Microsoft hasn't released an 'Xbox-version of Godot' or anything like that. Instead, it's essentially a demonstration of how to implement all of the necessary Xbox on PC requirements (such as Xbox services, PlayFab , and GameInput ) in a Godot-based game. "This sample shows you how to bring sign-in, gamepad controller support capabilities and more into your game with Godot. It includes insights, re-usable integration code, and is currently compatible with the April 2026 GDK out of the box," writes Microsoft in its dev blog post. Although Godot has been traditionally associated with 2D indie games, suc…

items.title: MSI's new Strike Alloy has almost every gaming keyboard buzzword thrown at it, with TMR, 8k, and rapid trigger, and you can even use it underwater
items.summary: H2Oh, that's kinda neat.
items.body: As gaming keyboards get more advanced, we have to learn new words just to understand them. We've had to learn what polling rates really mean and how much is too much for the average gamer (8k, btw), what the advantage of rapid trigger is, and why someone would want TMR. And not only has MSI thrown every single gaming keyboard buzzword at its new keyboard, but it dunked the keyboard underwater at this year's Computex to prove it will still work. That's as shown off by We Do Tech , who saw MSI plunge the keyboard. Reportedly, the wired keyboard still works even when submerged (though the water resistance may cut down your reaction time). That's a fun bit of marketing and a way of showing off how durable the keyboard is, though you are very unlikely to actually want to use the keyboard underwater. Still, at least I know if I spill water on it, it should be fine. The keyboard itself is pretty impressive, too. It has TMR switches, which offer super-low latency gameplay and very precise controls. Think Hall effect, but better. TMR (tunnelling magnetoresistance) also happens to be more power efficient, but that efficiency is lost on a wired keyboard. You get 8,000 Hz polling here, too, w…

items.title: Hyte's new prototype AIO cooler looks to pack funky RGB and transparent shrouds into an affordable package
items.summary: This might make an interesting option for a themed budget build.
items.body: Apart from adding new fans in unexpected places , experimenting with pump-less tech , or getting increasingly clever with mirrors and holograms , there's little you can do to spice up an AIO cooler. The basic tech, after all, is pretty settled. That doesn't stop some manufacturers from trying to add a little spice in whatever way they can, though, such as this new prototype that Hyte has been showing off at Computex. The main feature of note is that the shroud around the radiator fans is transparent. That means you can see through to the fins on the radiator, and it might just add a little more flair to your build if you're going for a transparent/translucent theme. The cap on the pump also looks nice—you can't tell from the still photo, but that RGB spins around, which looks great because the pump is lit up all over. We weren't given a name for the cooler, given it's just a prototype, but we were told that Hyte is aiming to have it start at $100. And although I'm not certain whether that would be for a triple-fan version pictured or a two-fan 240 mm one, I'd lean towards the former, given that's what they're actually showing off. The appeal, then, will be the combo of its price t…
